dialogueChunks.intro: It's done, {player_name}. The ones who did this have answered for their crimes.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} gathered the whole settlement to hear the news. You managed to {quest_action}, and the silence that followed was the kind that comes when a wound starts to close. {quest_stakes} can begin to heal now. Justice doesn't bring back the dead, but it lets the living start to move forward.
dialogueChunks.outro: Take this. It was meant for whoever set things right. You've earned it and then some.

playerResponses.text: I hope the watchman's family can find peace now.
Justice served. Take care of your people.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}. I owe you more than I can ever repay.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The streets are quieter now, but it's the good kind of quiet. The traders are coming back, and {target_npc} finally slept through the night. You didn't just {quest_action}: you restored something this settlement had lost. Safety. Confidence. The belief that {quest_threat} has consequences.
dialogueChunks.outro: We collected this from every household. Everyone wanted to contribute. You gave us back our courage.

playerResponses.text: Courage was already here. I just reminded you of it.
Glad the roads are safe again. That's what matters.

dialogueChunks.intro: The deed is done, and I won't pretend I feel nothing. I feel everything.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Vengeance is a strange thing, {player_name}. I expected to feel satisfied when you told me you'd managed to {quest_action}. Instead I just feel tired. {target_npc} said the same thing: the anger that kept us going just drained away. What's left is the work of rebuilding. But it's a task worth doing now, because {quest_threat} is gone.
dialogueChunks.outro: Here. You earned every bit of this. And know that what you did mattered, even if the feeling is more complicated than we expected.

playerResponses.text: Vengeance rarely feels the way we expect. But the living can move forward now.
The hard part is over. The rest is up to you.

dialogueChunks.intro: You're back. And from the look on your face, I'd say justice has been served.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} wants to name the rebuilt storehouse after the watchman. The whole settlement agreed within minutes. They're already carving the sign. {quest_stakes} won't be forgotten. And {quest_threat}? They'll be forgotten completely. That's the real punishment: oblivion for the guilty, memory for the innocent.
dialogueChunks.outro: Take this with the gratitude of everyone here. And if you ever need anything, you know where to find us.

playerResponses.text: A storehouse with his name on it. That's a legacy worth defending.
Good. He deserves to be remembered. I'll be on my way.
